metadata.convention: Rht-B1b/D1b GOF stabilised-DELLA alleles encoded as RHT_B1/RHT_D1 gm=2.0; Rht-B1c severe allele gm=2.5; mild Rht-B1d gm=1.5. Trap-3 tests (Rht + GA3) encoded with baseline='mutant' and expected='unchanged' (stabilised DELLA cannot be degraded by exogenous GA). Biosynthesis-KO + exogenous hormone rescues encoded baseline='mutant' expected='increased'. BR signalling KO (TaBRI1) + BL encoded baseline='mutant' expected='unchanged' (Trap-5-like). Environment treatments on source nodes (Gibberellin, Brassinosteroid, Auxin, Nitrogen) use exogenous_supply. Chemical inhibitors (PAC, Brz) encoded via mechanism_mapping to TAGA20OX or TADWF4 with gm=0.1. Homeolog-single KOs of redundant composites use gm=0.997.

perturbations.notes: Rht-B1b = GAIN-OF-FUNCTION stabilised DELLA (Rht-B1 homeolog B); encoded gm=2.0

perturbations.notes: TaGRF4 OE; Li et al. 2018 explicitly state GRF4 OE increases yield 'without increasing mature plant height'. TAGRF4 removed from network in refinement iteration 1 because its DELLA interaction is post-translational competition (protein binding), not transcriptional regulation of DELLA levels - an algebraic-framework mismatch. Test now untestable in this network.

evidence.title: Modulating plant growth-metabolism coordination for sustainable agriculture

perturbations.reconciliation_note: TaGRF4 removed from network.json in refinement iteration 1 - Li 2018 states OE does not increase mature height; DELLA interaction is post-translational competition not transcriptional, framework-mismatch. Retained in curated_edges.json as a DELLA-interacting factor.

evidence.authors: Phillips AL, Huttly AK, Alarcon-Reverte R, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA biosynthetic genes are essential for normal height; chemical inhibition with paclobutrazol (GA biosynthesis inhibitor) phenocopies ga3ox2 dwarf
evidence.claim: Paclobutrazol blocks GA biosynthesis and reduces height

perturbations.reconciliation_note: Paclobutrazol: GA-biosynthesis inhibitor (targets ent-kaurene oxidase, functionally reducing GA20ox output). Mapped to TAGA20OX gm=0.1

perturbations.notes: Brassinazole: inhibitor of DWF4 (BR biosynthesis). Mapped to TADWF4 gm=0.1

evidence.title: The brassinosteroid biosynthesis gene TaD11-2A controls grain size and its elite haplotype improves wheat grain yields
evidence.authors: Xu H, Sun H, Dong J, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: reduced BR biosynthesis resulted in smaller grains and stunted growth

perturbations.reconciliation_note: Brassinazole: inhibitor of DWF4 (BR biosynthesis). Mapped to TADWF4 gm=0.1

evidence.title: Induced variation in BRASSINOSTEROID INSENSITIVE 1 (BRI1) confers a compact wheat architecture
evidence.authors: Gill MS, Phillips AL, Tarkowska D, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: 24-epiBL treatment on WT wheat promotes BR signaling and elongation
evidence.claim: Exogenous brassinolide promotes stem elongation in WT

perturbations.reconciliation_note: Brassinolide (BL) treatment → Brassinosteroid exogenous_supply=1.0

perturbations.notes: Trap-5 analog: TaBRI1 triple KO + BL. BR receptor missing → exogenous BL cannot be perceived → unchanged vs mutant. Raw expected=decreased was reported vs WT.

evidence.title: Induced variation in BRASSINOSTEROID INSENSITIVE 1 (BRI1) confers a compact wheat architecture
evidence.authors: Gill MS, Phillips AL, Tarkowska D, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: Triple mutants produced severe dwarfism; BR application unable to rescue BR-insensitive phenotype
evidence.claim: TaBRI1 triple-KO insensitive to exogenous BR

perturbations.reconciliation_note: Trap-5 analog: TaBRI1 triple KO + BL. BR receptor missing → exogenous BL cannot be perceived → unchanged vs mutant. Raw expected=decreased was reported vs WT.

perturbations.notes: TaDWF4 overexpression → more BR biosynthesis → taller

evidence.title: Over-expression of TaDWF4 increases wheat productivity under low and sufficient nitrogen through enhanced carbon assimilation
evidence.authors: Milner MJ, Swarbreck SM, Craze M, Bowden S, Griffiths H, Bentley AR, Wallington EJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of TaDWF4-B in cv. Fielder led to significantly increased growth compared to the WT even under low N conditions

perturbations.reconciliation_note: TaDWF4 overexpression → more BR biosynthesis → taller

perturbations.notes: TaERF-A1 OE → activates PIF → taller

evidence.title: Natural variation in TaERF-A1 confers semi-dwarf and lodging-resistant plant architecture in wheat

evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of TaPIF4 produced significantly greater plant height and longer spike length
evidence.claim: TaERF-A1 OE increases plant height through TaPIF4

perturbations.reconciliation_note: TaERF-A1 OE → activates PIF → taller

perturbations.notes: TaERF-A1 JD6 LOF allele → less PIF activation → shorter

evidence.title: Natural variation in TaERF-A1 confers semi-dwarf and lodging-resistant plant architecture in wheat
evidence.authors: Li L, Sheng X, Zhang H, Chen J, Zhu R, Wang C, Ren Y, Lin Y, Chen D, Lv L, Zhang Q, Wang Y, Wang H
evidence.evidence_sentence: TaERF-A1JD6 protein accumulates at lower levels; Ser178-Phe (S178F) substitution influences the stability; considerably less effective at activating TaPIF4 expression, producing semi-dwarfism
evidence.claim: TaERF-A1 JD6 (F178S) natural variant reduces plant height

perturbations.reconciliation_note: TaERF-A1 JD6 LOF allele → less PIF activation → shorter
